verdict_reasoning: VEGFA is a secreted, heparin-binding disulfide-linked homodimer growth factor with no transmembrane domain or GPI anchor. It is constitutively secreted into the extracellular space where it binds VEGFR receptors on endothelial cells. Anti-VEGF therapeutics (bevacizumab, ranibizumab, aflibercept) target the circulating/soluble pool, not a cell-surface-anchored form of VEGFA itself. Applying the pre-no checklist: no documented stable wash-resistant TM-partner tethering; no documented PM minority pool via lysosomal exocytosis or cycling; no TM isoform at any lifecycle stage; no cell-death- or activation-induced outer-leaflet pool documented for VEGFA protein body; no germline/tissue-restricted surface display; pMHC is not the surface story. The secreted pool may transiently associate with surface HSPGs but this is reversible and washes off. The recruiter (VEGFR) is the surface entity; VEGFA itself remains secreted_only.
